event.summary: Entered validation with a deterministic command, instruction, graph, and device-impact plan.

acceptance.proof: A dependency-free contract requires a caller-selected nonzero maximum, rejects empty and oversized result batches before selection, and retains the original Vec allocation unchanged.
A single scan selects the result with the numerically minimum already finite RTT and retains its exact index, exchange, RTT, and OFS data without recomputation.
Equal minimum RTT values select the first input index as explicit Rusty LSL local candidate policy, not observed endpoint behavior or general filtering authority.

No exchange collection, packet, clock/timer read, I/O, UDP, retry, scheduling, periodic execution, offset history, correction/mapping, smoothing, dejitter, currentness, runtime activation, dependency, device, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

document.commit_policy: Commit the bounded selection contract separately from lifecycle acceptance and push preparation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route LSLC-002N bounded selection and strict runtime exclusions.
Synchronize public local-policy and non-scope prose.
Review public/private routing; no shared-skill mutation expected.
Review bounded selection and runtime authority separation; no shared-skill mutation expected.

non_scope: Exchange acquisition or collection, packet bytes/shapes, endpoint roles, clocks/timers, I/O, sockets, UDP, retries, scheduling, periodic execution, or provider evidence.

Implicit default count, statistical filtering beyond minimum RTT, offset history, timestamp correction/mapping, smoothing, dejitter, currentness, accuracy, reachability, interoperability, or runtime activation.
Dependencies, features, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts, black-box observation, or Manifold authority.
document.objective: Add the smallest dependency-free explicitly bounded nonempty selection contract over already accepted LSLC-002M formula results, retaining the original result Vec and selecting the first numerically minimum finite RTT under an explicit local tie policy without acquiring collection, clock, packet, mapping, or runtime behavior.
outputs: Nonzero batch limit and typed empty/oversize errors.
Allocation-preserving minimum-RTT selection with exact selected index and explicit first-tie behavior.
Public routing/architecture/validation documentation, Standard receipt, and exact-ID executed no-force feature-push receipt.
